sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem are in poor form, winless in their last 10 matches with 3 consecutive losses, conceding in their last 7 games. KRC Genk have had mixed results, with only 1 win in their last 5 matches but showing resilience with late goals in recent draws and losses. Genk's ability to score in tight situations could be key.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: KRC Genk dominate the head-to-head record, winning their last 8 matches against SV Zulte Waregem, including a 3-2 victory in their most recent encounter. Both teams often score in these fixtures, with 5 of the last 7 games seeing goals from both sides, suggesting a high-scoring potential.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem (12th, 23 points) struggle defensively, conceding 29 goals this season, while their attack has managed 26 goals. KRC Genk (9th, 25 points) have a similar goal tally (27 scored, 31 conceded), but their creative output, led by players like Jarne Steuckers (5 assists), gives them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Zulte Waregem, Jeppe Erenbjerg (8 goals) is a major threat despite the team's form slump. Genk’s Bryan Heynen (7.39 average rating, 4 goals in last 5 H2H matches) could be decisive in midfield, controlling the tempo against a faltering home side.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Genk's historical dominance and Zulte Waregem's ongoing struggles, the away side is slightly favored, aligning with Sofascore’s 40% probability for a Genk win. Expect a competitive match with goals likely on both ends, considering recent trends and defensive frailties.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between SV Zulte Waregem and KRC Genk ended in a goalless draw, with both teams displaying a mix of defensive solidity and attacking intent. Neither side managed to convert their chances, resulting in a 0-0 scoreline at the break. Zulte Waregem showed more promise in creating significant opportunities, while Genk dominated possession and territory. The game remains finely balanced as we head into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: SV Zulte Waregem
halftimeSections.text: SV Zulte Waregem had a decent first half, managing to create a notable chance but failing to capitalize on it. Their attacking play was more direct, with several entries into the penalty area and a healthy number of shots on target. Defensively, they held their own despite having less of the ball, winning a good share of duels. Players like Jeppe Erenbjerg and Anosike Ementa stood out for their contributions in the final third, showing potential to unlock Genk’s defense if given more space.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: KRC Genk
halftimeSections.text: KRC Genk controlled much of the possession in the first half and frequently ventured into the final third, though they struggled to create clear-cut chances. Their play was more methodical, focusing on building from the back, but lacked the final touch in the attacking areas. Defensively, they were tested by Zulte Waregem’s directness but managed to keep a clean sheet. Standout performers like Bryan Heynen and Matte Smets provided stability at the back and in midfield, which could be crucial in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Genk started strongly, maintaining consistent pressure in the opening minutes with several attacking spells. However, Zulte Waregem responded with a shift in momentum around the middle of the half, launching a series of dangerous moves that put Genk on the back foot. Towards the latter stages, momentum swung back and forth, with both teams having periods of dominance, though neither could sustain their attacking threat long enough to score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: Discipline will be key for Genk, as they’ve already picked up a caution in the first half, which could impact their approach if the game becomes more physical. Zulte Waregem’s ability to convert their chances will be critical, as they’ve shown they can penetrate Genk’s defense but need to be more clinical. For Genk, finding a way to translate their territorial dominance into meaningful opportunities will be essential if they are to break the deadlock. The midfield battle could also decide the outcome, with both teams needing to control that area to dictate play.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see an increase in intensity as both teams push for a breakthrough. Zulte Waregem may look to exploit any fatigue in Genk’s backline with their direct style, while Genk could capitalize on their superior possession to wear down the hosts. Substitutions could play a significant role, especially if fresh legs are introduced to change the dynamics of the game. Expect a more open contest as both sides take calculated risks to secure the win.
